title: Dangers of living beyond your means without financial management
description: BUSINESS NEWS - Most consumers live beyond...
bodyText:
BUSINESS NEWS - Most consumers live beyond their means where they have no financial management and spend more than they earn because the cost of living is very high.


> It is also easy to get caught up in the allure of a lifestyle beyond your means when it comes to expensive cars and extravagant vacations and even the simplest habits like eating out and excessive gifting.


“The consequences of living beyond our means can be extreme, leading to a cycle of debt, stress and uncertainty,” Capo Cassidy, change exchange author at Brightrock, says.

Remember, having a healthy relationship with your finances is more than just budgeting and saving. It is also about taking ownership of your financial well-being and making simple, significant choices that can have a significant impact on your future, Cassidy says and offers these four lessons to consider on your financial journey:


> Take responsibility and ownership for financial management


The first step towards financial wellness is acknowledging your current financial situation and taking ownership of it. It is essential to understand that financial circumstances are a result of the choices you made and that you have the power to change course by making better decisions moving forward.


> Simplicity in financial choices


Contrary to popular belief, managing your finances does not have to be complicated. By focusing on the fundamentals, such as budgeting, saving and avoiding unnecessary debt, you can lay a solid foundation for a secure financial future.

Making simple, good choices consistently can lead to significant improvements in your financial well-being over time.


> The power of small changes in financial management


It is easy to underestimate the impact that small changes can have on your finances. Whether it is cutting back on daily expenses, negotiating better deals on bills, or investing a little extra each month, these seemingly minor adjustments can add up to substantial savings and financial growth in the long run.


> Investing for growth


Building wealth requires more than just saving money. It requires investing wisely to achieve long-term growth. While investing can seem intimidating, especially for those new to the concept, it is essential to remember that there are various investment options available, catering for different risk tolerances and financial goals.

By educating yourself about investment strategies and seeking professional guidance when needed, you can harness the power of investments to secure your financial future.

Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
